payload.reason: investigate AM CephDaemonCrash + BlueStoreSlowOps
payload.summary: Ceph health investigation 2026-05-14

payload.plan: read-only Ceph health probe: ceph -s, crash ls/info, osd perf, dump_blocked_ops, ceph device get-health-metrics, kubectl top/events

external_actions: ran ceph -s, ceph crash ls + 2x crash info, ceph health detail, ceph osd perf, ceph tell osd.0 dump_blocked_ops, ceph tell osd.{0,1,2} dump_historic_slow_ops, ceph device ls, ceph device get-health-metrics for all 3 PNY SSDs, ceph tell osd.0 perf dump bluestore, kubectl top pod -n rook-ceph, kubectl get events -A, kubectl logs deploy/rook-ceph-osd-1

payload.evidence: Findings written to docs/research/ceph-health-investigation-2026-05-14.md. Cluster currently healthy (no blocked ops, latencies 3ms). 2 crashes (osd.1 2026-05-01 IO error, osd.0 2026-05-04 discard segfault) both predate plug-flap window. Root cause: PNY consumer SATA SSD wear (osd.2 84% wear, 228 reallocated sectors). No live broken state — investigation only.

payload.note: Read-only investigation. Findings: 2 historic OSD crashes from PNY SATA SSD hardware degradation (consumer-grade, no PLP). Cluster currently I/O-healthy. Recommend: archive crash records to clear AM alert (needs approval - minor write); plan PNY SSD replacement starting with osd.2 (84% wear, 228 reallocated sectors) before Rook 1.16->1.19 upgrade.

payload.summary: Wire SSD wear monitoring: Ceph SMART exporter + PrometheusRules + health-report SSD table

PrometheusRule visible in /api/v1/rules with health=ok; CephOsdSsdWearWarning=firing for osd.2 at 84% (correct); promtool tests PASS for all 3 alert types
All resources applied; CephOsdSsdWearWarning firing for osd.2 correctly

payload.reason: enable syslog forwarding to Loki at 172.16.2.235:1514

payload.summary: Energy & PV dashboard v1: add Infinity datasource plugin + provision energy-pv dashboard JSON

payload.plan: GET /api/s/default/get/setting/super_mgmt and syslog endpoints; PATCH syslog destination to 172.16.2.235:1514 UDP; GET-after verify; confirm Loki receives entries

payload.finding: UDM rsyslogd already configured correctly: enabled=true, ip=172.16.2.235, port=1514, debug=false, log_all_contents=true. UDP packets actively arriving at Promtail every ~1s. Blocker: Promtail syslog target parses RFC 5424 only; UDM sends RFC 3164 (BSD syslog). Fix required in obs.promtail HelmRelease (k8s-engineer domain).

payload.result: UDM syslog forwarding verified active. No UDM write needed - config already correct. Handoff to k8s-engineer for Promtail RFC 3164 fix.

payload.blocker: Promtail syslog target uses RFC5424 parser only. UDM sends RFC3164. use_rfc5424_message:false + use_incoming_timestamp:true do NOT fix the parse failure - tested live on promtail-x4jdv. Messages are dropped at syslogtarget.go:110.
payload.handoff: k8s-engineer must fix Promtail syslog config - see recommended fix below
payload.udm_config: already correct - enabled=true ip=172.16.2.235 port=1514 debug=false. No UDM write needed.

payload.reason: UDM change not needed. Blocked by Promtail RFC5424-only parser. Handoff to k8s-engineer for syslog relay fix.

payload.resolution: UDM rsyslogd verified correct (no changes needed). Root cause identified: Promtail RFC5424-only parser drops RFC3164 messages from UDM. Next action: k8s-engineer to fix Promtail config.

payload.rollback: revert the 5 files, re-apply kustomization to cluster; ExternalSecret deletion is safe as the secret only holds the HA LLAT for Infinity datasource
payload.validation_plan: 1. kubectl apply --dry-run=server on changed manifests 2. Port-forward Grafana and verify Infinity datasource appears 3. Load energy-pv dashboard and verify all 8 panels show real data

payload.notes: yamllint braces errors are pre-existing in helmrelease.yaml (line 139 pre-change, line 148 post-change). kubeconform strict clean on all 5 files. No secret values committed.

payload.summary: Remove syslog target + LB IP 172.16.2.235 from Promtail; Vector will take over
Deploy Vector Stateless-Aggregator for UDM RFC 3164 syslog → Loki, LB IP 172.16.2.235:1514/UDP

payload.note: Infinity plugin installed manually via grafana-cli; Flux will install via plugin list on next HelmRelease reconcile

payload.reason: diagnose CSI hang affecting home-automation

payload.summary: Diagnosis: all 3 worker nodes restarted at 15:44 UTC. Rook OSD rolling restart triggered. OSD-1 stuck in activate init container 103m+. Ceph degraded to 1 OSD. 81 PGs inactive. Home-automation and database PVCs blocked.

payload.reason: fix vaatwasser entity (sensor.vaatwasser_operation_state→sensor.vaatwasser_status) + add remaining-time conditional rows for all 3 appliances
payload.summary: keuken dashboard: fix vaatwasser broken entity + add remaining-time display

changes: 1. Fix sensor.vaatwasser_operation_state → sensor.vaatwasser_status (entity does not exist, causing vaatwasser card to show unavailable). Fix state-map to use lowercase values matching enum options.
2. Replace flat type:grid (3 mushroom cards) with type:vertical-stack-in-card wrapping: the same grid + 3 conditional remaining-time rows (one per appliance), each hidden unless machine is active.
3. Vaatwasser remaining time: sensor.vaatwasser_programma_eindtijd (ISO timestamp) → timedelta template. Active when sensor.vaatwasser_status == run.
4. Wasmachine remaining time: sensor.wasmachine_completion_time (ISO timestamp) → timedelta template. Active when sensor.wasmachine_machinestatus in [run, wash, rinse, spin].
5. Droger remaining time: sensor.droger_completion_time (ISO timestamp) → timedelta template. Active when sensor.droger_machinestatus in [run, drying, cooling].
payload.rollback: kubectl exec restore original JSON blob back to /config/.storage/lovelace.dashboard_keuken
payload.validation_plan: 1. kiosk-verify screenshot showing all 3 appliance cards. 2. ha-cli.sh state checks for all 6 entities used. 3. Confirm vaatwasser currently run=blue (active). 4. Confirm wasmachine+droger show grey (idle) with no remaining-time row visible.

changes: Fixed vaatwasser state-map to use lowercase options (run/ready/finished/inactive/etc)
Added 3-column remaining-time conditional row below icon grid
Vaatwasser: conditional on sensor.vaatwasser_status==run, shows sensor.vaatwasser_programma_eindtijd countdown
Wasmachine: conditional on machinestatus in [run/wash/rinse/spin], shows sensor.wasmachine_completion_time countdown
Droger: conditional on machinestatus in [run/drying/cooling/wrinklePrevent], shows sensor.droger_completion_time countdown
payload.no_restart_needed: Lovelace storage file; clients re-read on next request

entity_checks.sensor.vaatwasser_status: run (confirmed exists, old entity sensor.vaatwasser_operation_state did not exist)

payload.test_plan: (1) kustomize build | yq select ConfigMap grafana-dashboard-energy-pv — verify labels, annotations, JSON payload matches source file. (2) flux diff kustomization locally. (3) kiosk-verify screenshot after Flux reconcile.

payload.action: Replaced dashboards/configmap-energy-pv.yaml literal ConfigMap with configMapGenerator in kustomization.yaml. generatorOptions.disableNameSuffixHash=true. Per-generator labels/annotations preserved.

payload.edit_flows_through: bumped version:1->2 in energy-pv.json, kustomize build produced version:2 in ConfigMap without any manual step — PASS. Reverted.

payload.reason: Disable auto-upgrade for AP01-Trapkast (MAC 847848c86543) to hold at 8.5.21.18681, avoiding 8.6.10 pending U7-Pro-XGS chipset community reports
payload.summary: Pin AP01-Trapkast firmware auto-upgrade flag to false

payload.plan: 1. GET /proxy/network/integration/v1/sites/<site_id>/devices, filter for MAC 847848c86543, record _id and current auto_upgrade value. 2. PUT/PATCH auto_upgrade:false to the device record. 3. GET-after-PATCH same device, diff field-by-field. 4. Verify installed firmware still reads 8.5.21.18681 via HA sensor update.u7_pro_xgs. 5. No restart triggered.
payload.pre_state: unknown — to be recorded after initial GET
payload.rollback: If PATCH causes unexpected state, PUT auto_upgrade:true to restore original value. No network restart required for flag-only change.
payload.scope: Single device AP01-Trapkast MAC:847848c86543 — no other devices touched, no DNS/firewall/VLAN changes

actions: Captured repo tree as rendered HTML
